channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Birrell stuns fifth seed as Osaka and Sabalenka dazzle at French Open
item.description: Australia's Kimberly Birrell continues a day of upsets for Australia by advancing to the second round of the French Open for the first time in her career.

media:group.media:description: Kimberly Birrell has advanced to the second round of the French Open for the first time in her career.

item.title: Two men released without charge after Dezi Freeman-related arrest
item.description: A 48-year-old and a 45-year-old were taken into custody by detectives from Taskforce Summit, who continued to investigate Freeman's movements after the fatal shooting of officers Neal Thompson and Vadim de Waart-Hottart at a Porepunkah property in Victoria's north-east in August last year.

media:group.media:description: Dezi Freeman spent seven months on the run.

item.title: Outback school finally set to be rebuilt after mould forced demolition
item.description: More than two years since a health hazard led to Broken Hill's Willyama High School being knocked down, work on the "world-class" facility planned to replace is expected to begin.
item.dc:creator: Bill Ormonde

media:group.media:description: Tom Kennedy, Rebecca Milsteed, Local Government Minister Ron Hoenig, Barwon MP Roy Butler and Peter Macbeth turn the sod.

item.title: Hotspots reveal temperature danger zone for koala survival
item.description: The risks of death or hospitalisation of koalas increases as soon as seven-day average maximum temperature is above 27 degrees Celsius, a new study shows.
item.dc:creator: Peter de Kruijff

media:group.media:description: A koala tries to shelter from hot conditions in north-west NSW.

item.title: 'Not far away': Rescuers confident of reaching group trapped in Laos cave
item.description: Seven people remain trapped after heavy rain triggered a landslide but rescuers say they are close to reaching the group.

media:group.media:description: Rescuers have been working for days to free the trapped group.

item.title: The extraordinary legacy of a trailblazing female driving instructor
item.description: One of South Australia's first female licensed driving instructors has been remembered for her tireless advocacy and devotion to her community.
item.dc:creator: Jackson Byrne

media:group.media:description: Margaret Greenslade speaking at Renmark High School in 1972, where she officiated as a representative member of the school council in inducting the school prefects.

item.title: Town sites closed for testing after kids find liquid mercury vials at tip
item.description: A south-west Queensland town's school, service station, hospital waiting room and several homes are closed for testing after children found liquid mercury vials at the tip and took them to school.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Walsh

media:group.media:description: Cunnamulla State School is expected to remain closed until at least Thursday.

item.title: Employment services to get largest overhaul in 30 years
item.description: Jobseeker services will shift away from a one-size-fits-all approach to a new tiered model, to be unveiled by the government later today.
item.dc:creator: Holly Tregenza

media:group.media:description: The government's proposed new system will include three tiers of support for different types of job seekers.

item.title: NT paramedics consider body-worn cameras amid ongoing violent assaults
item.description: The Northern Territory's ambulance service is considering introducing police-style body-worn cameras after the arrest of alleged murderer Jefferson Lewis led to rioting in Alice Springs last month.
item.dc:creator: Tilda Colling

media:group.media:description: St John Ambulance NT is considering putting further security measures in place to ensure the safety of their paramedics.

item.title: BP removes chair over 'serious concerns' about conduct
item.description: The BBC reported Albert Manifold's departure followed concerns about "bullying" and his "overbearing" behaviour, something he later described as a "false narrative".
item.dc:creator: Tessa Flemming

media:group.media:description: Albert Manifold has been removed from his role eight months into his tenure.

item.title: 'Kidding ourselves': Daley whacks talk of Cleary's losing Origin record
item.description: NSW coach Laurie Daley says the responsibility does not solely rest on the shoulders of his star No.7 ahead of this year's State of Origin campaign.

media:group.media:description: The pressure is on Nathan Cleary to reverse a losing Origin career record as this year's campaign gets underway.

item.title: Live: Westpac fined $26m for 'grossly negligent' conduct, ASX heading for cautious start
item.description: The Federal Court imposes a $26 million fine on Westpac for failing to respond quickly to customers facing financial hardship, while the Australian share market is likely to start its day relatively flat after Iran accused the United States of violating its fragile ceasefire and warned of retaliation. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: David Chau

media:group.media:description: Westpac's agreed $1.3 billion fine is almost twice the previous record corporate penalty paid by CBA.

item.title: Student says WA school closed by toxic baits had been overrun by mice
item.description: A year 12 student at the WA College of Agriculture Morawa says there were fears of "cooking" mice in the heating system before the mistaken deployment of toxic baits shut the school down.
item.dc:creator: Chloe Henville

media:group.media:description: The deployment of toxic mouse baits has closed the WA College of Agriculture in Morawa.

item.title: Rain brings potential 'opportunities' in renewed search for Gus Lamont
item.description: Eight months since Gus Lamont was reported missing, police have again returned to search Oak Park Station as part of an ongoing investigations into the four-year-old's disappearance.
item.dc:creator: Jordanna Schriever

media:group.media:description: Police have launched a renewed search at Oak Park Station for missing four-year-old Gus Lamont.

item.title: Scott reveals he wanted to trade Merrett, confirms bumper Reid offer
item.description: The ousted Essendon coach reveals he was against the decision to keep Zach Merrett at the club despite a late trade offer from Hawthorn.

media:group.media:description: Brad Scott (left) revealed he wanted Essendon to trade Zach Merrett (right) to Hawthorn last summer.

item.title: Australian underdog stuns former world number one at French Open
item.description: Queenslander Adam Walton makes it through the heat and five-set grind to down former world number one Daniil Medvedev at the French Open.

media:group.media:description: Adam Walton celebrates with the crowd after downing Daniil Medvedev at the French Open.

item.title: After two decades of addiction, drug court offered Brandie a way out
item.description: The peer support worker at Dubbo's Bunmabunmarra service knows firsthand how the drug court can offer a second chance.
item.dc:creator: Lily Plass

media:group.media:description: Brandie McGrady says she has turned her life around in the past two years.

item.title: Live: Calls to raise CGT concession threshold to $10m for small business
item.description: Small Business Minister Anne Aly says consultation with start-ups and small businesses is ongoing, but doesn't give much away as the peak small business body urges the government to expand capital gains tax concessions. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Gould

media:group.media:description: Anne Aly is advising people not to turn to memes for advice.

item.title: Two prospective Whyalla steelworks buyers remain, as sale reaches 'final stages'
item.description: The federal and state governments have reduced the number of bidders for the financially stricken Whyalla steelworks from five to two.

media:group.media:description: Only two bidders remain in the race to buy the Whyalla steelworks.

item.title: 'Can't put it into words': Origin star's mum reveals heartbreaking call
item.description: Cameron Munster's mum reveals the heartbreaking call she had to make before her superstar son led Queensland to victory days after the shock death of his dad.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Kelly

media:group.media:description: A tearful Queensland coach Billy Slater hugs skipper Cameron Munster after last year's Origin decider.

item.title: Iran calls latest US strikes 'bad faith' sign as internet slowly restored
item.description: Iran has denounced US strikes in the country's south as a sign of "bad faith and unreliability" as negotiations toward a possible deal to end the war continue.

media:group.media:description: Israel has increased strikes and ground operations in Lebanon.

item.title: ACT public school system to be unified in 'generational shift'
item.description: The ACT's public education system will be significantly reformed after a review of resourcing found schools operated much like "independent small businesses".
item.dc:creator: Monte Bovill

media:group.media:description: The review of public school system resourcing recommended implementing a unified approach to the system.

item.title: Rare hybrid turtle hatchlings discovered on Queensland beach
item.description: Rare baby turtles that are a cross between the loggerhead and green species have been found on the beach at Mon Repos.
item.dc:creator: Nikki Sorbello

media:group.media:description: Two of the 15 hybrids found have been sent to Sea World for further research.

item.title: 'No excuse' for renewable company destroying Wiradjuri rock shelter
item.description: Members of a First Nations community in Central West NSW say they were devastated to learn a culturally significant rock shelter had been destroyed to make way for a renewable energy project.
item.dc:creator: Lani Oataway

media:group.media:description: The rock shelter near the Cope State Forest in the Mudgee district that was destroyed by ACEREZ workers.

item.title: Murderer convicted of 1999 crime back in custody for breaching parole
item.description: Murderer Garry Thompson has been returned to custody for 12 months for breaching his parole conditions multiple times.
item.dc:creator: Jordanna Schriever

media:group.media:description: Garry Thompson was jailed for life by South Australia's Supreme Court for the 1999 murder of Jodi-Ann Trueman.
